The breach was announced in a forum thread dated June 19, 2026, with a sample of the data provided to verify its authenticity.  The sample entries reveal detailed personal and professional information, with records primarily concentrated in Delhi and surrounding regions. Fields in the dataset include names alongside corresponding mobile numbers, Gmail and Yahoo email addresses, and specific residential or business addresses. The data also categorizes individuals by industry sectors such as banking, retail, engineering, and hospitality. A direct download link via a file-sharing service and a Telegram handle were provided by the anonymous poster, indicating the data is being actively distributed or sold.  The scale of this leak is substantial, encompassing tens of millions of individuals. Exposure of this volume of PII creates severe risks for targeted phishing campaigns, spam calls, social engineering attacks, and identity theft. For the affected individuals in India, the breach underscores critical vulnerabilities in data protection practices and highlights the ongoing threat of large-scale credential and PII harvesting on underground markets. The Threat Actor sent a notification to hundreds of thousands of people which read "misanthropi4".  More details have emerged regarding the compromise, and truthfully, it is terrifying. The Brazilian government is extremely secure and it is quite remarkable any living person could have gotten past their enhanced security measures.  "mizanthropiaz" found a username and password login to the Brazilian governments Emergency Alert System. The username and password was present because an employee working there accidentally infected themselves with malware in 2016.  The employee there did not change the password in over 10 years.  But, after the employee accidentally infected their computer with malware, did they change their password? No.  But, did the Brazilian government do IP address blacklisting which would prevent unauthorized devices from accessing the Emergency Alert System? No.  But, did the Brazilian government require a VPN connection to authenticate to the Emergency Alert System because it's a government network? No.  But, did the Brazilian government require MFA (e-mail, text, or Authenticator Code)? No.  But, did the Brazilian government send notifications on new devices connecting? No.  But, did the Brazilian government issue alerts on password changes or password change requests? No.  But, did the Brazilian government require e-mail verification prior to changing or resetting passwords? No.  But, did the Brazilian government have rate-limiting to prevent brute-force attacks? No.  But, did the Brazilian government introduce a CAPTCHA to prevent brute-force attacks? Yes, but it was 2+2 and it never changed. It always asked "2+2="  But, did the Brazilian government require password complexity to make brute-forcing difficult? No, the password to the Brazilian government employee was the same as their username.

sample_tweets.text: Ah. I think it was  nitter.privacyredirect.com/SwiftOnSecurity/status…

This should remove a large part of the historical BYOVD driver pool on systems that actually reach enforcement. Good move.  But WHCP signing is still not a security review. At @nextronresearch, we regularly find WHCP-signed drivers with obvious privilege-escalation flaws. Microsoft now controls the trust choke point and should use it for automated vulnerability screening - static and dynamic analysis, dangerous IOCTL checks, maybe LLM-assisted triage before signing.  As Michael points out here, reaching enforcement isn’t automatic. A cross-signed driver that repeatedly loads and would fail the new policy can keep resetting the evaluation period, potentially preventing the system from ever reaching enforcement.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-23 08:19 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 모델과 자동화된 워크플로우 플랫폼(Dify, Langflow 등)의 취약점을 악용하여 텐트 간 데이터 탈취 및 RCE를 시도하는 경향이 뚜렷합니다. 또한, 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)이 WordPress 플러그인과 NPM 패키지 전반으로 확산되고 있으며, 인포스틸러(Infostealer)를 통해 수집된 대규모 계정 정보가 인터넷에 무방비로 노출되는 사례가 빈번합니다. 마지막으로, EDR을 무력화하는 프레임워크와 함께 합법적인 도구(ClickOnce, RMM)를 악용한 다단계 피싱 공격이 고도화되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **The Gentlemen**: EDR Killer 프레임워크를 운용하는 RaaS 그룹으로, 공격적인 채용 전략을 통해 활동 범위를 빠르게 확장 중.
*   **FishMonger**: SprySOCKS for Windows 백도어를 사용하여 커널 드라이버를 악용하는 등 고도의 은닉 기술을 구사함.
*   **North Korean Hackers (Kimsuky 등)**: Mastra NPM 패키지 공급망 공격 및 보안 담당자를 타겟으로 한 스피어 피싱 캠페인 수행.
*   **Scattered Spider**: Transport for London(TfL) 사이버 공격에 연루되어 법적 처벌 절차 진행 중.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **AryStinger 봇넷**: 한국 내 구형 D-Link 공유기를 감염시켜 공격 중계지로 악용하는 사례가 다수 발견됨.
*   **공급망 공격**: Chai.js 플러그인으로 위장한 악성 NPM 패키지(`chai-as-init`)가 국내 개발 환경을 타겟으로 배포됨.
*   **금융권 피싱**: 2026년 5월 기준, 국내 금융권 대상 초기 침투 경로로 피싱(Phishing) 공격이 가장 높은 비중을 차지함.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2024-4577**: PHP-CGI (Windows), CVSS 9.8, RCE 취약점, 악용 사례 다수.
*   **CVE-2026-49468**: LiteLLM, CVSS 9.5, 인증 우회, Exploit in the wild.
*   **CVE-2026-8461**: FFmpeg (PixelSmash), CVSS 8.8, Heap OOB Write, RCE 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-47729**: Squid Proxy (Squidbleed), CVSS 미상(High급), 정보 유출.
*   **CVE-2026-55447**: Langflow, 인증 토큰 탈취 및 RCE 가능.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **도메인**: 없음 (뉴스/블로그 내 특정 C2 도메인 미명시)
*   **해시**: 없음 (뉴스/블로그 내 특정 파일 해시값 미명시)
*   **CVE**: 
    *   CVE-2026-8461 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/ffmpeg-fixes-pixelsmash-flaw-in-widely-used-video-decoder/))
    *   CVE-2026-47729 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/decades-old-squid-proxy-flaw-squidbleed-can-expose-user-data/))
    *   CVE-2026-49468 ([KaitanSecurity](https://sec.kaitan.id/cves/CVE-2026-49468/))
    *   CVE-2026-55447 ([MalwareBibleJP](https://twitter.com/MalwareBibleJP/status/2069193220719071283))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **패치 및 업데이트**: 사용 중인 FFmpeg, Squid Proxy, LiteLLM, Langflow 버전을 즉시 최신으로 업데이트하십시오.
*   **공유기 보안 점검**: D-Link 등 구형 공유기 사용 환경을 전수 조사하고, 펌웨어 업데이트가 불가능한 기기는 네트워크 격리 또는 교체하십시오.
*   **공급망 보안 강화**: NPM 패키지 의존성 관리 시 `chai-as-init` 등 의심스러운 패키지 포함 여부를 확인하고, 개발 파이프라인 내 시크릿 관리(GitHub Token 등) 정책을 재점검하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: 공급망 공격과 AI 플랫폼 취약점이 결합되어 실질적인 RCE 및 데이터 탈취가 빈번하게 발생하고 있으며, 국내 인프라(공유기)가 직접적인 타겟이 되고 있음.
